data.title: Delta Air Lines Is About to Report Its Q4 Earnings Tomorrow. Options Traders Are Expecting a 7.04% Move in DAL Stock
data.description:
Delta Air Lines (DAL), an American carrier, is set to release its Q4 results tomorrow, January 13. DAL stock has gained 11% in 2025, primarily driven by steady travel demand and stronger corporate bookings. According to TipRanks' Options Tool, options traders expect about a 7.04% move in either direction for DAL stock in reaction to Q4 2025 results.  This implied move is lower than DAL stock's average post-earnings move (in absolute terms) of 12.2% over the past four quarters.

data.title: "Prove-It Year": Tesla Stock (NASDAQ:TSLA) Gains as Tesla Faces Deadlines
data.description:
Give electric vehicle giant Tesla (TSLA) credit for trying. It has imposed several major deadlines on itself this year, leading some to call 2026 Tesla's "Prove-It Year." That is a lot of pressure to put itself under, and improves the odds that it will disappoint somewhere along the line. Still, as the old saying says, if you aim for the moon and miss, the worst that happens is you end up among the stars. Investors took that notion to heart, and sent Tesla stock up nearly 2% in Monday afternoon's trading.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

This is a big year for Tesla. Advances in robotics, in self-driving technology, and in new vehicle design are all set to show up in 2026. The Roadster is expected to make a comeback, with an April 1 reveal date just in case something goes amiss. Cybercab volume production is set to kick in.

All of this sounds great, but there is one major concern as far as analysts are concerned: can Tesla's planned autonomous operations, backed up by artificial intelligence, actually scale? This led to Morningstar analyst Seth Goldstein declaring, "I think 2026 is going to be the prove-it year for Tesla's robotaxi business. That's ultimately going to be the major driver for Tesla this year."

Another Door Lawsuit

Meanwhile, Tesla's gee-whiz door handles—particularly the aspects that make them difficult if not outright impossible to operate in an emergency—are drawing lawsuits again. A new class-action lawsuit alleges that the door handles on some Model S cars "routinely fail" after just a few years. One complaint noted that a 2015 Ludicrous variant owner saw three out of four door handles fail, requiring the driver to enter the vehicle from the passenger side, which featured the only remaining working door.

Apparently, a group of Tesla owners whose cars are 10 to 12 years old are discovering that the door handles are among the first things to go. This leaves owners unable to get in the vehicle they own, and may in turn prompt a major safety risk. Tesla, meanwhile, did not respond.

Is Tesla a Buy, Hold or Sell?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on TSLA stock based on 13 Buys, 10 Holds, and seven Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 10.34% rally in its share price over the past year, the average TSLA price target of $405.94 per share implies 10.2% downside risk.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Top BofA Analyst Sees 'Upside to Street Estimates' for Apple's Q1 Earnings, Reaffirms Buy Rating on AAPL Stock
data.description:
Ahead of iPhone maker Apple's (AAPL) results for the first quarter of Fiscal 2026 on January 29, top Bank of America analyst Wamsi Mohan reaffirmed a Buy rating on AAPL stock with a price target of $325. The analyst expects Apple to deliver "upside" to the Street's estimates, driven by continued strength in iPhone sales and double-digit growth in the company's Services revenue.

data.title: Former Fed Chairs Warn Eroding Fed Autonomy Carries 'Highly Negative Consequences'
data.description:
On Monday, several former Fed Chairs and Treasury Secretaries, including Janet Yellen, Ben Bernanke, and Alan Greenspan, issued a joint statement in response to the Department of Justice's investigation into Fed Chair Jerome Powell.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

"The reported criminal inquiry into Federal Reserve Chair Jay Powell is an unprecedented attempt to use prosecutorial attacks to undermine that independence," read the statement.

Why is Powell Being Investigated?

The statement added that waning Fed independence could result in "highly negative consequences" for inflation and the economy, similar to previous situations seen in emerging markets.

The Justice Department's investigation stems from Powell's oversight of the central bank's $2.5 billion renovation of its Washington, D.C., headquarters. In a response to the probe, Powell framed the renovation as a pretext for political attacks on the Fed's independence amid a feud with Trump, who has repeatedly called for lower rates.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Trump Suggests Using SpaceX to Restore Internet Access in Iran
data.description:
President Donald Trump said that he plans to speak with Elon Musk about restoring internet access in Iran, where the government has imposed a near-total internet blackout during widespread anti-government protests. Indeed, Trump told reporters in Washington that Musk is well-suited to help because of his experience running large satellite networks. He pointed to Musk's company SpaceX and its Starlink satellite internet service as a possible solution, saying that Musk has "a very good company" that could bring connectivity back to the country.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Iran's shutdown has made it extremely difficult for people to communicate and share information as protests have spread across the country. In the past, Starlink has been used in Iran during similar periods of unrest, which has allowed some users to bypass government restrictions and access the global internet. While neither Musk nor SpaceX has commented on Trump's latest statement, Musk has previously provided Starlink access in Iran during periods of protest.

At the same time, Trump's comments come as his relationship with Musk has recently improved after a public disagreement last year over domestic policy. The two have recently appeared together again, including at Trump's Mar-a-Lago resort. Nevertheless, the blackout in Iran has caused international concern, with rights groups estimating hundreds of deaths and thousands of arrests, although exact numbers are hard to confirm because communication inside the country remains heavily restricted.

What Is the Prediction for TSLA Stock?

When it comes to Elon Musk's companies, most of them are privately held. However, retail investors can invest in his most popular company, Tesla (TSLA). Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on TSLA stock based on 13 Buys, 10 Holds, and seven Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average TSLA price target of $405.94 per share implies 10.2% downside risk.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Dow Jones Today: DJIA Wavers on DOJ Probe of Powell as JPMorgan Warns of 'Near-Term Underperformance'
data.description:
The Dow Jones (DJIA) is trading slightly lower on Monday as investors digest the implications of the trump">Department of Justice's investigation into Fed Chair Jerome Powell.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Over the weekend, Powell announced that he was subject to a probe over the Fed's $2.5 billion renovation of its Washington, D.C., headquarters. However, he believes the true reason for the investigation relates to the Fed setting interest rates independently and without political bias.

"The threat of criminal charges is a consequence of the Federal Reserve setting interest rates based on our best assessment of what will serve the public, rather than following the preferences of the President," Powell said in a statement.

President Trump has repeatedly called for lower interest rates while criticizing Powell for delaying rate cuts. That's led to concern that the autonomy and independence of the central bank are at risk, with several former Fed Chairs and Treasury Secretaries addressing this issue in a joint statement on Monday. "The reported criminal inquiry into Federal Reserve Chair Jay Powell is an unprecedented attempt to use prosecutorial attacks to undermine that independence," read the statement.

The investigation into Powell also creates an "overhang" for stocks that could result in "near-term underperformance" as investors react to the risk of waning Fed independence, said Andrew Tyler, JPMorgan's Global Head of Market Intelligence. Tyler added that a de-escalation of threats to the Fed's independence could create a buying opportunity given that the economy and corporate fundamentals remain healthy.

The Dow Jones is down by 0.14% at the time of writing.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Is Tom Lee 1 Million ETH Move a Genius Play for BitMine Stock?
data.description:
To help you decide whether the Tom Lee's 1 Million ETH move is truly a genius play for BitMine stock, we have to look at the logic behind this massive milestone. When a company "stakes" its crypto, it isn't just sitting on a pile of digital gold. It is effectively taking its assets and ethereum">putting them into the engine of the Ethereum (ETH-USD) network to keep it running. For BitMine Immersion Technologies (BMNR), hitting the 1 million ETH milestone is the equivalent of a real estate mogul buying up 1 million apartment units. They aren't just waiting for the land value to go up; they are collecting rent every single day.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The Pros: Turning Volatility into a Paycheck

In the regular world, if you own a stock and the price doesn't move, you make $0. BitMine has changed that math for their business.

  • The Cash Flow Machine: Even if the price of Ethereum stays exactly the same all year, BitMine is on track to earn roughly $94 million in new ETH. This gives the company breathing room to pay staff and keep the lights on without having to sell their core investment.
  • The Network Effect Power: As one of the largest holders, BitMine now has a seat at the table. They have a vested interest in making Ethereum better, faster, and more secure, because their entire business depends on that one ecosystem staying healthy.

The Cons: BitMine's Golden Handcuffs

Every economic move has a cost, and for BitMine, that cost is flexibility. Economics is the study of trade-offs, and here are the ones they are making:

  • The Success Trap: BitMine is now married to Ethereum. If a faster or better technology comes along and people stop using Ethereum, BitMine's $3.3 billion investment could lose value rapidly. They can't easily shift to a new trend because their wealth is literally locked in the Ethereum vault.
  • The Liquidity Risk: In a crisis, speed is everything. If the market starts to crash, BitMine cannot just click "sell." There is often a waiting period to unstake or unlock those tokens. In a fast-moving market, being unable to move your money for days or weeks can be the difference between surviving and going bust.

Dilution Risks Exist

While the company is stacking ETH, it is also looking to "stack" more shares, and this is where investors need to be most careful. The chairman, Tom Lee, has proposed a massive increase in the number of allowed shares, jumping from 50 million all the way to 50 billion.

Management claims this is merely to allow for future stock splits, which would keep the price looking affordable at around $25 per share. However, this move effectively hands the company a blank check. This power allows them to print and sell new shares whenever they need extra cash, which can shrink the percentage of the company you own. It is like a pizza being cut into thousands of tiny slivers; the actual pizza doesn't get any bigger, but your individual slice becomes much thinner over time.

At the time of writing, Ethereum is sitting at $3,112.75.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Meta Stock Down as Italian Watchdog Stops Exclusion of Rival Chatbots from WhatsApp
data.description:
Meta's (META) shares trended lower on Monday following reports that the U.S. tech giant has taken a step to comply with the order from the Italian competition watchdog. The order barred it from excluding rival AI chatbots from its messaging platform WhatsApp.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Italy Pushes against Move to Block Rival AI Chatbots

In October, business solutions terms to WhatsApp, guiding how businesses and developers use WhatsApp's business features, including the AI features. However, AGCM, the Italian competition authority that has been investigating Meta since July to determine if it abused its dominant position in the social media market, ordered Meta to jettison the updated terms.

At the time, a spokesperson for the social media firm said the company would appeal the decision. However, according to a notice seen by Reuters, Meta last week informed AI providers in the European country that they will be exempted from following the company's new terms that will enforce the exclusion.

This suggests Meta might have changed its mind in that regard.

Meta Faces EU Probe on AI Policy

AGCM's order in late December came even as European regulators in the same month launched an antitrust probe into Meta's AI policy on WhatsApp.

This came after Meta AI, which was introduced in September 2023, finally launched in Europe in March, following months of regulatory obstacles. In a separate case, recent reports also indicated that the EU was looking to make Meta follow stricter content moderation rules.

Meanwhile, Meta continues to integrate its AI products into its family of apps and recently even rolled out a new Business AI solution for third-party websites.

Is META Stock a Good Stock to Buy Now?

On Wall Street, Meta's shares continue to command a Strong Buy consensus rating from analysts. This is based on 37 Buys, six Holds, and one Sell issued by 44 analysts over the past three months.

In addition, the average META price target of $824.71 implies about 27% upside from current trading levels.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: U.S. Stocks Face 'Near-Term Underperformance' on Fed Independence Risks, Says JPMorgan
data.description:
The trump">Department of Justice's investigation into Fed Chair Jerome Powell has sparked concerns that the central bank's independence is at risk. That could be problematic for U.S. stocks, according to JPMorgan (JPM).

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

"The risk around Fed independence is likely to push the US toward near-term underperformance," said Andrew Tyler, JPMorgan's Global Head of Market Intelligence.

DOJ Probe Poses Risk to Fed Credibility, Stocks

The Fed's primary goal is to maximize employment and keep inflation contained. However, bowing to political pressure could undermine its credibility and ability to manage the economy effectively.

Tyler said that the investigation creates an "overhang" for U.S. stocks, despite a positive macroeconomic backdrop and strong corporate fundamentals. He added that a buying opportunity could emerge if the Trump administration eases its challenge to Fed independence.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Home Depot Stock (NYSE:HD) Gains With New Housing Plans
data.description:
Home improvement giant Home Depot (HD) has been eagerly awaiting interest rate cuts for some time now, in the hopes that this will spur homeowners to take out cheap loans again and upgrade their living spaces. But while the Federal Reserve seems less than enthusiastic about the idea, a new plan from the Trump administration may be ready to step in. The news gave Home Depot stock a fractional boost in Monday morning's trading.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Instead of waiting for the Fed to move—which is proving to be a bit of a lost cause—Trump is instead turning to a pile of cash reserves at Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac to buy mortgage-backed securities. This move, reports suggest, will help drive down mortgage rates and, in turn, spur new homebuilding as more customers are willing to take on loans at lower interest rates.

This would also likely drive up homeowner projects, as they would either fix up their spaces ahead of sale, or just fix up their spaces to remain therein. This in turn is a huge deal for Home Depot, which counts on a lot of this business for day-to-day operations.

Trouble Down the Street

But this kind of program also helps other home improvement stores like Lowe's (LOW), and Lowe's is about to put some significant improvement into at least some of its stores. Some Lowe's locations will include what reports are calling a "rural showroom," the fifth of which is going into a Lowe's in Manning, South Carolina.

The "rural showroom," the reports noted, will include key points for rural dwellers, including outdoor equipment, livestock feed, automotive merchandise, and more. While the "rural showroom" concept is still very much in its early stages—Manning's is the first in South Carolina—it is also the kind of thing that could probably be exported to other locations quickly. In turn, it could also distinguish Lowe's from Home Depot, and pull revenue accordingly.

Is Home Depot a Good Long-Term Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on HD stock based on 17 Buys, five Holds and one Sell assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 3.74% loss in its share price over the past year, the average HD price target of $401.47 per share implies 6.81% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson
